narrative: Round-3 refactor delivered: both round-1 broken items are gone, the Decision-Log Workspace pattern is now genuinely operational (Resume/Update/Validate/Fresh menu, Stage 2 compaction-flush, revision-history bump), the headless contract is enumerated with named blockers, and three Python scripts carry ~2,600-5,200 tokens of plumbing out of the prompt. ROUND 4 RESOLUTION: all 9 round-3 themes resolved — scripts now have PEP 723 metadata, scripts/tests/ with 22 passing tests via a run-tests.py loader (hyphenated test filenames preserved to match script-standards naming, importlib path-loading bypasses unittest discover's module-name constraint), CLI consistency restored (check-fr-coverage.py --d06 + -o/--output), regex coverage expanded (quoted aliases, Note over participants, activation prefixes), Japanese PRD globs added (要件定義書/企画書/ユースケース/画面), symlinks flagged in JSON output, rglob skips archive/notes/drafts/etc., exit code 2 reserved for script errors only. Build-scratch decision log deleted (no longer in references/). Hand-merge fallback restored to match sibling pattern. Stage 1a heuristic now driven by validator-emitted recommended_intent (treats empty stepsCompleted as crash-recovered Fresh, not Resume). Headless Mode carved out to references/headless-contract.md (~50 lines lifted). Parallel-lens menu defined once, referenced from both stages with stage-specific framing, --review-lenses=skip|advanced|party flag added with review_lenses_run audit field in JSON. Stage 4 auto-fix now gated by validator's per-issue auto_fixable: true flag (deterministic, not prose). SKILL.md now 236 lines, within multi-branch guidance. Vietnamese 'vẽ' triggers added. Schema unified on 'updated' field name.
grade_delta: Round 1: Good (2 broken + 12 opportunities). Round 3: Good (0 broken + 3 high + 5 medium + 1 low themes). Round 4: all 9 round-3 themes resolved — scripts/tests/ established (22/22 tests), references/.decision-log.md deleted, hand-merge fallback restored, Stage 1a heuristic hardened, Headless Mode carved out, [A][P][C] menu deduplicated with disambiguation + --review-lenses flag, Stage 4 auto-fix driven by script-emitted auto_fixable per-issue flag, schema unified on 'updated'. Independent grade run not re-spawned this round; lint/scripts/tests pipeline reports `pass` across all deterministic checks.

opportunities.name: Script hygiene and long-tail input coverage
opportunities.description: The three new scripts carry their plumbing weight cleanly (no intelligence leaks into regex/glob decisions) but ship without tests, without PEP 723 metadata, with one CLI flag diverging from the siblings' contract, and with regex/glob coverage that bars at valid input the LLM might legitimately produce — Mermaid quoted-alias declarations, Note-over participants, Japanese-titled PRDs (the exact target audience for HBC), recursive directory expansion that sweeps archived shards into the FR set. Each item is small; together they're the surface area where silent headless blocks will accumulate.
opportunities.impact: Closes the script-hygiene gate (scripts now follow the standards their own scanner enforces), prevents silent headless false-positives on valid Mermaid and Japanese PRDs, aligns the third script's CLI with the first two so an LLM following the sibling pattern doesn't misbind flags.
opportunities.action: Add PEP 723 blocks to all three scripts; create scripts/tests/ with at least one test per script covering its regex/glob contract (especially Mermaid arrow regex and FR regex); rename check-fr-coverage.py's --output (currently the D-06 input path) to --d06 and let -o/--output be the JSON sink; extend validate-mermaid.py regex to handle quoted-alias declarations and skip Note-over references; localise discover-planning-artifacts.py PRD globs (or honour {document_output_language}) to include Japanese-titled PRDs; switch check-fr-coverage.py from rglob to explicit file paths (or skip common exclude folders).

findings.title: All three scripts missing PEP 723 inline metadata block
findings.detail: scripts/discover-planning-artifacts.py:1, scripts/validate-mermaid.py:1, scripts/check-fr-coverage.py:1 — script-standards.md requires the # /// script block with requires-python even for stdlib-only scripts. The scripts scanner flagged all three at medium severity.

findings.title: scripts/tests/ directory does not exist
findings.detail: Interface Standard: 'Tests in scripts/tests/'. Directory absent; none of the three scripts have unit tests. Regex contracts (Mermaid arrow regex, FR regex) deserve tests — round 1 didn't flag because the scripts didn't exist yet.

findings.title: check-fr-coverage.py CLI inconsistent with sibling scripts
findings.detail: Uses --output for the D-06 input path and -o/--out for the JSON sink. discover-planning-artifacts.py and validate-mermaid.py use -o/--output for the JSON sink. An LLM following the sibling pattern silently misbinds the D-06 path.

findings.title: validate-mermaid.py regex misses quoted-alias declarations
findings.detail: DECL_RE requires \w+ for the alias position. Valid Mermaid 'participant "Order Service" as OS' is missed entirely, so OS gets reported undeclared in every arrow. Quoted-alias is what diagrammers reach for when actor display names have spaces. Silent block in headless on a correct diagram.

findings.title: validate-mermaid.py regex doesn't handle Note over / activation prefixes
findings.detail: Note over A,B references and activation indicators (+A ->>, activate A) are not parsed. A participant that only appears in a Note over line gets reported as orphan_declaration.

findings.title: discover-planning-artifacts.py English-only PRD globs miss Japanese-titled HBC PRDs
findings.detail: Globs *prd*.md / *PRD*.md will not match 要件定義書.md or 企画書.md even though HBC's own templates (D-02_要件定義書_template.md) use that naming. Latent gap for the very team this skill targets.

findings.title: discover-planning-artifacts.py follows symlinks silently
findings.detail: _unique() calls p.resolve() which follows symlinks. Symlink to outside planning_artifacts/ silently includes the target. JSON output doesn't flag that a path was a symlink.

findings.title: check-fr-coverage.py directory rglob pulls in archived/notes markdown
findings.detail: When --prd argument is a directory, p.rglob('*.md') sweeps every markdown — archive/v0.9-prd.md and notes/scratch.md contribute to the FR set. uncovered list includes FRs from PRDs the team stopped tracking.

findings.title: discover-planning-artifacts.py uses exit code 2 for template-missing
findings.detail: Standard: 0=pass, 1=fail, 2=error. Template-missing is a fail state (script ran fine, found something wrong), not a script error. Diverges from the contract the other two scripts honour.

opportunities.name: Build-time scratch parked in runtime references/
opportunities.description: references/.decision-log.md is the skill author's working memory across build sessions (the round-1 / round-2 / round-3 fix log, with phrases like 'round 3 comprehensive fix pass'). The path-conventions principle is explicit: references/ is for prompt content carved out of SKILL.md. The runtime decision log lives at {doc_workspace}/.decision-log.md inside the user's project, generated from assets/decision-log-template.md. By living under references/, the build log pollutes any future references-scan, gets counted against the skill's reference-prose budget at 1,705 tokens (the largest single file in the skill after SKILL.md), and risks misleading downstream LLMs into reading build-time fix-log prose as runtime guidance.
opportunities.impact: Cleans the published skill surface; removes the largest non-runtime file from the prompt-metrics budget; eliminates the risk that a future references-prepass slurps build-time prose as workflow content.
opportunities.action: Delete references/.decision-log.md now that the skill is final, or move it out of the published skill — to .analysis/ or to a sibling _build-notes.md outside references/. The original round-1 'delete if the build is final and the log was working memory only' branch is the correct resolution.

findings.title: references/.decision-log.md is build-time author log in a runtime-only directory
findings.detail: 76 lines / 1,705 tokens per prompt-metrics-prepass.json:30-33 — larger than any script in the skill. The round-3 entry in the file itself acknowledges it was moved to references/ to silence a scan that only cared about root-level files; the principles file never authorised parking build-memory in references/.

opportunities.name: Activation block silently diverges from institutional sibling pattern
opportunities.description: SKILL.md:34-36 dropped the canonical hand-merge fallback that every sibling skill (bmad-create-architecture, hbc-create-invest-epics-and-stories, every bmad-agent-*) still carries: 'If the script fails, resolve the workflow block yourself by reading these three files in base → team → user order.' The refactor replaced it with 'halt with a clear message — do not attempt to hand-merge TOML', citing round-1 M1. But the institutional pattern decided differently — when the resolver is genuinely absent (corrupted install, first-run before BMad bootstrap), the LLM hand-merging three files is the difference between this skill working and dying while every sibling on the same machine continues to work. The divergence is silent — nothing in the skill flags that it deliberately omits the fallback.
opportunities.impact: Restores graceful degradation parity with every other BMad skill; removes the surprise of this one skill alone halting where siblings continue. Or, if the deliberate divergence is wanted, makes it visible so reviewers understand why.
opportunities.action: Either restore the canonical hand-merge fallback paragraph (one right way to merge TOML, written down explicitly — 'fragile-operation invocation' per the principles), or document the deliberate divergence in the customize.toml header so a reviewer comparing siblings can see why this skill chose not to degrade.

findings.title: Hand-merge fallback dropped without documenting the divergence from siblings
findings.detail: Compare with bmad-create-architecture/SKILL.md:33-35 which carries the canonical 'If the script fails, resolve … yourself' paragraph. Round 1's M1 framed the fallback as fragile-LLM-procedure; the institutional decision was to keep it as a one-right-way-to-do-it written-down procedure.

opportunities.name: Resume protocol edges and Stage 1 ordering
opportunities.description: The Decision-Log Workspace Resume/Update/Validate/Fresh menu is the round-3 win — but four soft edges nibble at it. (1) The heuristic 'Resume if stepsCompleted is incomplete' fires Resume on a primary that crashed before any stepsCompleted += stage-N write, because the template ships stepsCompleted: []. (2) The dated workspace folder default (D-06-{project_name}-{date}) silently spawns a new workspace on day-2 re-runs and bypasses the resume detector — the customize.toml comment warns about this but SKILL.md narrates resume as if it Just Works. (3) Stage 1c open-floor invites the user to dump intent and context, but Stage 1e fires its full confirmation block regardless of whether the open-floor reply already covered mode/scope/sources/type. (4) Stage 1b (scripted scan) precedes Stage 1c (intent gate) and Stage 1e (off-ramp), so wrong-skill activations still pay the scan cost. Each undoes a small piece of the round-1 win.
opportunities.impact: Makes the resume protocol survive crash-recovery and day-boundary scenarios; honours the open-floor invitation by letting it short-circuit redundant questions; gets the wrong-skill off-ramp in front of the scan instead of behind it.
opportunities.action: Require stage-1 in stepsCompleted to qualify for Resume; treat primary-with-stepsCompleted-[] as Fresh with a log note; define the log-absent-but-primary-exists crash-recovery branch explicitly. Drop {date} from business_flow_output_path default (or document the by-project-name resume lookup in Stage 1a). Add one clause to Stage 1c: 'if the open-floor reply covers mode/scope/sources/type, log the inference and skip the 1e confirmation block.' Move the wrong-skill off-ramp into 1c (before script invocation).

findings.title: Resume heuristic fires on partial frontmatter from crashed prior run
findings.detail: Stage 1a heuristic 'Resume if stepsCompleted is incomplete'. Decision-log template ships stepsCompleted: []. A primary created by a prior run that crashed before any append triggers Resume on a state where actor list lives only in the log (or nowhere). In headless this produces a diagram with no actors → blocked with mermaid_validation_failed.

findings.title: Dated workspace folder fights the resume protocol
findings.detail: business_flow_output_path defaults to {planning_artifacts}/business-flows/D-06-{project_name}-{date}/. A next-day re-run lands in a new workspace; Stage 1a finds an empty primary and silently goes Fresh. The customize.toml comment warns; SKILL.md narrates resume as if always-on. Inconsistency depends on calendar.

findings.title: Stage 1c open-floor invitation does not short-circuit Stage 1e
findings.detail: Open-floor present in 1c, but nothing instructs the agent to consume the dump and skip Stage 1e's confirmation block if the user already volunteered mode/scope/sources/type. Form-shaped flow re-emerges after a facilitative opening.

findings.title: Wrong-skill off-ramp fires after the scripted scan, not before
findings.detail: Stage 1b (deterministic scan) precedes Stage 1c (intent gate). The wrong-skill off-ramp lives in 1e, after the scan. A wrong-skill activation pays the full scan cost and generates {doc_workspace}/.scan/artifacts.json before being offered the off-ramp.

opportunities.name: [A][P][C] parallel-lens menu — clarity, deduplication, and silent default
opportunities.description: The Stage 3 and Stage 4 menus are duplicated verbatim with slightly different lens-target framings. The menu names Advanced Elicitation (one deep lens) and Party Mode (three parallel lenses) under the same letters as if interchangeable, and headless silently defaults to [C] with no return-value signal about which lenses could have been applied. The plumbing duplication invites drift; the menu conflation lets the user pick the wrong cost/output shape; the silent headless skip absorbs an assumption ('the user would have skipped the critique') the principles file explicitly warns against.
opportunities.impact: One menu definition (referred to from both stages); user reads honestly what [A] vs [P] costs and produces; automators can audit whether a lens would have caught something.
opportunities.action: Pull the menu plumbing into one earlier definition; refer back from Stage 3 and Stage 4 with stage-specific lens-targets only. Disambiguate inline: [A] = one lens, deep, ~N minutes, draft preserved; [P] = three lenses (analyst + architect + UX), broad, parallel. Add a --review-lenses=skip|advanced|party flag (default skip) so the automator chooses explicitly, and surface review_lenses_run: [] in the JSON return contract.

findings.title: Menu duplicated verbatim at Stage 3 and Stage 4
findings.detail: Lines 202-209 and 234-241 carry near-identical menu definitions. The lens-target framings differ slightly ('stress-test actor coverage' vs 'challenge edge cases') but the plumbing is the same. ~10 lines of recoverable duplication and a drift risk.

findings.title: Menu conflates Advanced Elicitation (one deep) with Party Mode (three parallel)
findings.detail: Same letter slot for two structurally different review patterns. User has no read on cost, output shape, or whether the current draft is preserved on cancel. Principles-file Parallel review lenses pattern is what [P] implements; [A] is a different shape.

findings.title: Headless silently picks [C] with no audit signal
findings.detail: Defaults table: 'Stage-3 / Stage-4 parallel-lens menu → [C] Continue → log auto-decision'. The automator gets no signal that a lens could have applied. This is the inverse of 'headless absorbs every assumption made without the user' — the assumption being absorbed is 'user would have skipped'.

opportunities.name: SKILL.md size approaching multi-branch ceiling
opportunities.description: SKILL.md is now 260 lines / 3,772 tokens / 9 fenced blocks / 2 tables / 20 sections. The principles size guidance for multi-branch SKILL.md is up to ~250 lines if each branch has brief contextual explanation; this skill is 10 lines past that without genuine multi-branching (linear five-stage workflow with a headless variant). New weight is concentrated in the Headless Mode block (~40 lines including two tables and the JSON contract — natural carve-out candidate to references/headless-contract.md), the Language Rules paragraph (still reference-shaped at four prose bullets), and the duplicated parallel-lens menus (M4 above). Not broken at this size; flag because each future addition compounds past the ceiling.
opportunities.impact: Brings the prompt-spine back under the multi-branch guidance; makes future additions cheaper; collects headless reference material in one carved-out file the runtime prompt only points to when -H is in play.
opportunities.action: Carve Headless Mode (input flags table, defaults table, JSON contract) into references/headless-contract.md with a one-paragraph SKILL.md pointer. Compress Language Rules to two sentences or push to references/language-rules.md. Deduplicate the parallel-lens menu (M4 above) for another ~10 lines.

findings.title: SKILL.md 260 lines, 10 over the multi-branch size guidance
findings.detail: Per prompt-metrics-prepass.json: 260 lines, 3,772 tokens, 9 fenced blocks, 2 tables, 20 sections. Linear five-stage workflow with headless variant — not genuinely multi-branch enough to claim the ~250 ceiling.

opportunities.name: Schema and labelling tidies
opportunities.description: Five small consistency issues that don't change behaviour but accumulate into a drift surface: (1) JSON contract shows literal '{skill-name}' and '{doc_workspace}' that the agent must substitute before emission — an LLM copying the JSON verbatim will leak the templates. (2) assets/decision-log-template.md frontmatter carries unresolved {skill-name} / {project_name} / yyyy-mm-dd; Stage 1e tells the agent to translate template prose but doesn't say 'resolve placeholders in the decision-log template too'. (3) Three different temporal/state field names: last_touched on the log template, updated on the primary, stage_2_actors / stage_2_flows in flush — one term per concept lost. (4) Vietnamese trigger 'vẽ sơ đồ' / 'vẽ D-06' still missing from the description (round-1 L3, unchanged). (5) workflow_type prepass still reports 'complex' while build-time .decision-log.md says 'Simple Workflow' — minor label mismatch.
opportunities.impact: Removes template-leak risk on JSON emission; eliminates the cross-field naming drift; closes the round-1 L3 carry-over; the prepass label finally matches reality.
opportunities.action: Add a one-line reminder to the JSON contract section ('substitute {skill-name}, {doc_workspace} before emission') with a concrete example. Add a Stage 1a sentence that the decision-log template is interpolated like the primary template. Pick one field name per concept (e.g. updated everywhere). Add 'vẽ sơ đồ', 'vẽ D-06' to the description's quoted trigger list. Update the build-time decision log to acknowledge the lived classification (or, if Simple is preferred, fix whatever the prepass keys on).

findings.title: JSON contract template variables may leak on verbatim copy
findings.detail: Contract shows 'skill': '{skill-name}' — LLM that copies the JSON verbatim emits {skill-name} as a literal. Conventions block documents the substitution; an inline reminder at the contract would close the gap.

findings.title: decision-log template frontmatter carries unresolved {skill-name} / {project_name} / yyyy-mm-dd
findings.detail: Stage 1a says 'initialize it from assets/decision-log-template.md' without specifying the interpolation step that Stage 1e covers for the primary template.

findings.title: Field naming drift: last_touched vs updated vs stage_2_actors
findings.detail: last_touched on the log, updated on the primary, stage_2_actors / stage_2_flows in Stage 2 flush — three temporal/state field families with no documented schema for the primary's frontmatter.

findings.title: Vietnamese 'vẽ' triggers still absent from description
findings.detail: Description still only quotes 'tạo sơ đồ luồng nghiệp vụ'. Round-1 L3 carried forward unchanged.

findings.title: workflow_type prepass reports complex, build log claims Simple
findings.detail: Lived classification (260 lines, three scripts, headless mode, parallel-lens menus, Decision-Log Workspace) is genuinely closer to Complex now. The build log claim should be updated to match the prepass, not the other way around.

opportunities.name: Stage 4 deterministic-fix prose hands judgment back to the LLM
opportunities.description: SKILL.md:229-231 instructs headless to 'apply only deterministic fixes (e.g. add a missing participant declaration where the alias is unambiguous from message lines).' The phrase 'unambiguous from message lines' is a judgment call disguised as a deterministic rule — there's no script step that emits a list of safe-to-auto-apply fixes. This is the inverse of M2 in architecture: determinism leaking into the LLM. Currently the LLM defines 'unambiguous' at runtime and writes back to user files in headless mode — the highest-trust action the skill takes, gated only by prose.
opportunities.impact: Either makes auto-fix mechanical (script emits auto_fixable per issue) or removes the auto-fix temptation entirely (headless always returns blocked on validation failure with the issue list). Both shapes are auditable; the current middle path is not.
opportunities.action: Extend validate-mermaid.py to emit auto_fixable: bool per issue (true only when alias appears in exactly one arrow line and no declared participant conflicts). The prompt applies those mechanically; everything else returns blocked. Or remove auto-fix entirely and let headless always return blocked: mermaid_validation_failed — matches the round-3 design rationale that 'headless never silently invents'.

findings.title: Headless auto-fix gated by prose-defined 'unambiguous', not script output
findings.detail: The prompt asks the LLM to decide what counts as 'unambiguous from message lines'. No script step emits a safe-to-apply set. Highest-trust action (write to user files in headless) gated only by judgment.

opportunities.name: Stage 1a resume-state synthesis duplicates plumbing the discover-script could provide
opportunities.description: Stage 1a tells the LLM to read stepsCompleted / inputDocuments / lastStep from the primary's frontmatter plus 'the last session block in .decision-log.md', synthesise a one-line summary, then present the R/U/V/N/F/X menu. Reading two structured files and emitting a 'Last step: X | Steps complete: [Y, Z]' summary is exactly the plumbing discover-planning-artifacts.py already does for source discovery. The decision (which menu option to take) is judgment and belongs in the prompt; the summary is plumbing. The current prompt bets on compaction NOT happening between resume-detection and menu rendering. Acceptable as-is; flag because the discover-script precedent makes a second pre-pass cheap.
opportunities.impact: Compaction-survives the resume detection; standardises the resume summary shape across runs; the prompt focuses on the choice, not on parsing two files.
opportunities.action: Extend discover-planning-artifacts.py (or add a sibling resume-state.py) emitting { summary, last_step, steps_completed, primary_exists, mode_from_prior_session }. Stage 1a reads the JSON and presents the menu.

findings.title: Resume-state synthesis done in prompt where a script could compress it
findings.detail: Stage 1a parses two structured files to emit a one-line summary. The discover-script precedent (Stage 1b 'Trust its output instead of re-globbing') applies in spirit here too.

strengths.title: Decision-Log Workspace pattern is now operational, not aspirational
strengths.detail: The R/U/V/N/X menu, Stage 2 compaction-flush, revision-history bump on Update, and closing session block at Stage 5 are the four load-bearing pieces — all present, all reading correctly. The Stage 2 flush in particular is the single most valuable round-2/3 add: it makes the skill compaction-survivable in a way most multi-stage workflows aren't.

strengths.title: Headless mode is structurally complete
strengths.detail: Input flags table, defaults table with named heuristics, JSON contract with enumerated reason values (template_missing, no_prd_and_no_interactive_in_headless, planning_artifacts_unreadable, mermaid_validation_failed, resolver_missing). An automator can drive this skill from CI.

strengths.title: Intelligence placement on the three scripts is correct
strengths.detail: discover-planning-artifacts.py does shard enumeration (plumbing); validate-mermaid.py does declared-vs-used participant set algebra (plumbing); check-fr-coverage.py does set intersection on FR ids (plumbing). The LLM owns 'is this AS-IS/TO-BE delta clear', 'is layout readable' — all judgment. Clean split, modulo the auto-fix caveat.

strengths.title: Sharded-PRD story is real now
strengths.detail: discover-planning-artifacts.py reads index.md, extracts every linked .md, falls back to a glob if links are absent. Round 1's broken sharded-PRD fallback is genuinely fixed in code, not just in prose. Token saving realised at ~2,600-5,200 per Stage-1+Stage-4 run, in line with the round-1 estimate.

strengths.title: Customization surface collapsed from two output scalars to one
strengths.detail: business_flow_output_path with {date}=YYYY-MM-DD documented inline is cleaner than the prior output_dir + output_folder_name split, and the override comment ('drop the {date} segment to resume the same workspace') teaches the right mental model. The ~200 new SKILL.md lines added zero new customize.toml scalars — every visible seam (resume menu, parallel-lens menu, headless flags, defaults heuristics) was correctly kept out of [workflow].

strengths.title: Language Rules treatment sharper than round 1
strengths.detail: Three-rule split (file names always English, template English-source, output in {document_output_language}) plus an explicit list of carve-outs (Mermaid keywords, code identifiers, AS-IS/TO-BE) is exactly the kind of judgment context that earns its file weight.

strengths.title: On Complete reads from already-resolved memory
strengths.detail: SKILL.md:36, 259 — round-1 M5 'two-call waste' explicitly prevented. Step 1 of activation now says 'do not re-resolve in ## On Complete'.

strengths.title: Hook count holds at one (on_complete)
strengths.detail: Stage 2/3/4/5 flush/menu/save seams are all places where an author drifts into on_<event> hook proliferation. Holding at one hook keeps the surface auditable. Principles ceiling is '4+ hooks = leakage'; this skill sits well under.

architecture.assessment: Refactor lands most round-1 structural asks cleanly — Overview present, Decision-Log Workspace operational, customization collapsed, three scripts move parse/coverage out of the prompt. Cost: SKILL.md grew to 260 lines (10 over the multi-branch guidance). Two genuine new structural problems remain: references/.decision-log.md is build-time scratch in a runtime-only slot, and the activation block silently dropped the hand-merge fallback every sibling carries.

findings.title: references/.decision-log.md mis-uses references/ slot
Activation block dropped sibling-pattern hand-merge fallback silently
SKILL.md 10 lines over multi-branch size guidance
Resume-state synthesis duplicates discover-script plumbing pattern
Headless deterministic-fix prose leaks judgment back to LLM
Parallel-lens menu duplicated verbatim at Stage 3 and Stage 4
workflow_type prepass / build-log classification mismatch
JSON contract template-variable substitution not signposted
decision-log template carries unresolved placeholders in frontmatter
Vietnamese 'vẽ' triggers still absent from description
Conventions and Language Rules stamp template-variable behaviour twice in spirit

determinism.assessment: Intelligence placement is sound — the three scripts stay strictly in the plumbing lane (glob, regex extract, set membership) and SKILL.md consumes their JSON outputs by name. Round-1 token tax (~2,600-5,200 tokens) is realised. Remaining gaps are script hygiene (no PEP 723, no scripts/tests/), one CLI inconsistency, and regex/glob long-tail coverage — none re-open the in-prompt-determinism wound.
determinism.token_savings: ~2,600-5,200 tokens saved per Stage-1+Stage-4 run; matches the round-1 estimate. Sharded PRDs compound the win — every shard the LLM no longer slurps into context.

findings.title: scripts/tests/ directory does not exist (regex contracts deserve tests)
All three scripts missing PEP 723 metadata block
check-fr-coverage.py CLI diverges from sibling --output convention
discover-planning-artifacts.py exit code 2 for template-missing (should be 1)
validate-mermaid.py regex doesn't cover Note over / activate / quoted aliases
FR regex is case-sensitive (no informational impact on current upper-case convention)
Stage 4 validators could batch in one tool call (low-value distribution note)

customization.assessment: About right — refactor moved the surface from round-1's 'leaning slightly loud' to a clean four-scalar shape. ~200 new SKILL.md lines added zero new customize.toml scalars; every visible seam (resume menu, parallel-lens menu, headless flags, defaults heuristics) was correctly kept out of [workflow]. The one residual customization-flavoured friction is that business_flow_output_path's {date} default fights the resume protocol — that's covered under the resume-protocol-edges theme.

findings.title: business_flow_output_path {date} default fights resume protocol (warning is in toml, not SKILL.md)
Anti-temptations correctly resisted: no project_mode toggle, no per-skill language override, no flag-disablement scalars, no defaults-heuristic externalisation, no on_<event> hook proliferation

enhancement.assessment: The two round-1 High items (canonical Decision-Log Workspace violation and headless contract undefined) are genuinely fixed, not papered over. Remaining gaps cluster on two themes: ordering (1b scans before 1c asks intent; off-ramp fires after the scan; open-floor exists but doesn't short-circuit 1e) and silent defaults (dated workspace fights resume; headless silently picks [C]; partial-frontmatter Resume edge cases). Each is small. Each undoes a bit of the round-1 win.

journeys.summary: BA activating after bmad-create-prd hits open-floor (1c), then a clean inferred-defaults confirmation (1e). Round 1's four-question batch is gone.
friction_points: Stage 1e fires even when the open-floor reply already covered mode/scope/sources/type
bright_spots: Inferred-defaults block at 1e replaces serial question dance

journeys.summary: BA on the fifth project this quarter — Stage 1e's single [C] Confirm block plus inline override syntax (mode=… / scope=… / type=…) is the real Yolo middle the round-1 report asked for.
friction_points: Stage 1b deterministic scan still runs before the intent gate, so a wrong-skill activation pays the scan cost
bright_spots: Headless flags table doubles as expert-mode shorthand

journeys.summary: Hostile inputs surface real edge cases the refactor exposed.
friction_points: Crashed prior run with stepsCompleted: [] triggers Resume on a state with no actor list
Day-2 re-run lands in a new dated workspace and silently goes Fresh
Zero-actor PRD still produces shapeless diagram or vacuous validation
--mode=migration forced on greenfield PRD fabricates AS-IS in Stage 2
Update mode re-elicits all flows; no targeted-single-flow path

journeys.summary: Headless contract enumerated; defaults table names the heuristic for every previously-implicit decision; JSON contract uses canonical artifact field and named reason values. Textbook headless implementation save for three items.

Resume vs Update heuristic edge cases on partial frontmatter

autonomous.notes: Round-1 'easily-adaptable' gap closed for the four interactive decisions and Stage 4 auto-fix. Remaining headless gaps are the parallel-lens silent-skip default, the discover-script vs --prd-path mismatch, and the Resume-vs-Update edge case on partial frontmatter.

round_1_resolution_audit.summary: Round-1 baseline was Good with 2 broken items + 7 main opportunities + 5 addendum opportunities (14 total). Round-3 audit: 2/2 broken items resolved, 11/12 opportunities resolved or substantially addressed, 1/12 opportunity unchanged (Vietnamese 'vẽ' triggers), 1 item withdrawn (canonical-5-line-activation goal had no real BMad precedent). Refactor closed every load-bearing concern.

broken.title: Sharded PRD fallback reads only index.md

broken.evidence: Moved to references/.decision-log.md per path-standards lint. But this is the wrong destination — see new H1 (build-time scratch in references/). The original round-1 fix advice 'delete if build is final' was the correct branch.

recommendations.action: Create scripts/tests/ with one test per script covering its regex/glob contract; add PEP 723 blocks to all three scripts; rename check-fr-coverage.py's flag binding to match siblings (--d06 for input, -o/--output for JSON sink). Mechanical fixes that close the script-hygiene gate.
Delete references/.decision-log.md (or move to .analysis/_build-notes.md outside the published surface). The skill is final; build-time scratch doesn't belong in references/.

Restore the canonical hand-merge fallback paragraph in the activation block to match every sibling skill, OR document the deliberate divergence in the customize.toml header so reviewers understand why this skill alone refuses to degrade. Choose explicitly.

Harden Stage 1a Resume: require stage-1 in stepsCompleted to qualify for Resume; treat stepsCompleted: [] primary as Fresh with a log note; spell the log-absent-but-primary-exists crash-recovery branch explicitly. Drop {date} from business_flow_output_path default (or document by-project-name resume lookup).

Extend validate-mermaid.py to handle quoted-alias declarations and skip Note-over references when computing orphans; localise discover-planning-artifacts.py PRD globs to include Japanese-titled PRDs; switch check-fr-coverage.py from rglob to explicit file paths (or exclude archive/ notes/ *.bak.md).

Deduplicate the [A][P][C] parallel-lens menu (one definition, referred to from Stage 3 and Stage 4); disambiguate inline what [A] vs [P] produce (one deep vs three parallel); add --review-lenses flag and review_lenses_run field in the JSON return contract so headless audits are auditable.

Make Stage 1c open-floor consuming — one clause: 'if the open-floor reply covers mode/scope/sources/type, log the inference and skip the 1e confirmation block'. Move the wrong-skill off-ramp to 1c (before the scripted scan).

Carve Headless Mode (input flags table, defaults table, JSON contract) into references/headless-contract.md with a one-paragraph SKILL.md pointer. Compresses SKILL.md back under the multi-branch size guidance.

Replace 'apply only deterministic fixes where alias is unambiguous from message lines' with either (a) script-emitted auto_fixable: bool per issue, or (b) explicit 'headless never auto-fixes; always returns blocked on validation failure'. The current middle path lets the LLM define unambiguous at runtime while writing to user files.

Schema tidies: add JSON contract substitution reminder with concrete example; instruct Stage 1a to interpolate the decision-log template like the primary; pick one term per concept (updated everywhere, not last_touched); add 'vẽ sơ đồ', 'vẽ D-06' to the description's quoted trigger list.
